name: Veal Stock - Master Chefs
recipeIngredient: 6 lb Bones, veal, meaty (OR combination of veal and beef bones)
2 cups Water, plus more as needed
2 large Carrots, peeled, trimmed, coarsely chopped
1/2 teaspoon Thyme, dried or 3 sprigs Thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Place oil in a roasting pan and heat briefly in the oven.
3. Add bones to the oil in the pan, toss to coat and roast for 35 minutes.
4. Add onions, carrots, celery, leek, garlic, and parsley, tossing them all to coat with fat. Roast for an additional 30 minutes.
5. Remove pan from the oven and transfer bones and vegetables to a clean stockpot. Drain off as much of the fat as possible.
6. Place the roasting pan over medium-high heat (use two burners if necessary). Add 2 cups cold water, bring to boil briefly, and scrape up all browned bits into the water.
7. Transfer liquid to the stock pot and add enough cold water to cover. Bring slowly to a boil, skimming froth that forms.
8. Lower heat and add tomatoes, thyme, bay leaves, cloves, and salt. Simmer uncovered for 6-8 hours, adding water as necessary just to cover ingredients. Skim whenever needed.
9. Add peppercorns during last 15 minutes of simmering.
10. Strain "soup" through a colander lined with double-layer dampened cheesecloth into a large bowl. Gently press solids to extract liquid and discard solids.
11. Pour stock into containers for storage, labeling and dating them. The stock keeps up to 3 days in refrigerator and up to 6 months in freezer.
